news.title: 🇪🇸 pedro sánchez is positioning himself as a leader of a global socialist revival, vowing to “twist
news.summary:
🇪🇸 pedro sánchez is positioning himself as a leader of a global socialist revival, vowing to “twist the arms” of far-right forces.

according to politico, sánchez used a left-wing summit in barcelona to 랠리 key leftist leaders against the rise of the far right and to strengthen his international profile.

at the summit, he accused donald 트럼프 of under채굴 multilateral institutions through 관세s and the war in the middle east. 

he also claimed that while far-right groups are organizing internationally, their numbers reflect “weakness,” not strength.

sánchez is reportedly seeking to expand his influence in europe and is eyeing the position of president of the european council, currently held by antónio costa.

news.title:
very interesting reading


palantir (@palantirtech)

because we get asked a lot.

the technological
news.summary:
very interesting reading


palantir (@palantirtech)

because we get asked a lot.

the technological republic, in brief.

1. silicon valley owes a moral debt to the country that made its rise possible. the engineering elite of silicon valley has an affirmative obligation to participate in the defense of the nation.

2. we must rebel against the tyranny of the apps. is the iphone our greatest creative if not crowning achievement as a civilization? the object has changed our lives, but it may also now be limiting and constraining our sense of the possible.

3. free email is not enough. the decadence of a culture or civilization, and indeed its ruling class, will be forgiven only if that culture is capable of delivering economic growth and security for the public.

4. the limits of soft power, of 급상승ing rhetoric alone, have been exposed. the ability of free and democratic societies to prevail requires somETHing more than moral appeal. it requires hard power, and hard power in this century will be built on software.

5. the question is not whETHer a.i. weapons will be built; it is who will build them and for what purpose. our adversaries will not pause to indulge in theatrical debates about the merits of developing technologies with critical military and national security applications. they will proceed.

6. national service should be a universal duty. we should, as a society, seriously consider moving away from an all-volunteer force and only fight the next war if everyone shares in the risk and the cost.

7. if a u.s. marine asks for a better rifle, we should build it; and the same goes for software. we should as a country be capable of continuing a debate about the appropriateness of military action abroad while remaining unflinching in our commitment to those we have asked to step into harm’s way.

8. public servants need not be our priests. any business that compensated its employees in the way that the 연준eral government compensates public servants would struggle to survive.

9. we should show far more grace towards those who have subjected themselves to public life. the eradication of any space for forgiveness—a jettisoning of any tolerance for the complexities and contradictions of the human psyche—may leave us with a cast of characters at the helm we will grow to regret.

10. the psychologization of modern politics is leading us astray. those who look to the political arena to nourish their soul and sense of self, who rely too heavily on their internal life finding expression in people they may never meet, will be left disappointed.

11. our society has grown too eager to hasten, and is often gleeful at, the demise of its enemies. the vanquishing of an opponent is a moment to pause, not rejoice.

12. the atomic age is ending. one age of deterrence, the atomic age, is ending, and a new era of deterrence built on a.i. is set to begin.

13. no other country in the history of the world has advanced progressive values more than this one. the united states is far from perfect. but it is easy to forget how much more opportunity exists in this country for those who are not hereditary elites than in any other nation on the planet.

14. american power has made possible an extraordinarily long peace. too many have forgotten or perhaps take for granted that nearly a century of some version of peace has prevailed in the world without a great power military conflict. at least three generations — billions of people and their children and now grandchildren — have never known a world war.

15. the postwar neutering of germany and japan must be undone. the defanging of germany was an overcorrection for which europe is now paying a heavy price. a similar and highly theatrical commitment to japanese pacifism will, if maintained, also threaten to shift the balance of power in asia.

16. we should applaud those who attempt to build where the market has failed to act. the culture almost snickers at musk’s interest in grand narrative, as if billionaires ought to simply stay in their lane of enriching themselves . . . . any curiosity or genuine interest in the value of what he has created is essentially dismissed, or perhaps lurks from beneath a thinly veiled scorn.

17. silicon valley must play a role in addressing violent crime. many politicians across the united states have essentially shrugged when it comes to violent crime, abandoning any serious efforts to address the problem or take on any risk with their constituencies or donors in coming up with SOLutions and experiments in what should be a desperate bid to save lives.

18. the ruthless exposure of the private lives of public figures drives far too much talent away from government service. the public arena—and the shallow and petty assaults against those who dare to do somETHing other than enrich themselves—has become so unforgiving that the republic is left with a significant roster of ineffectual, empty vessels whose ambition one would forgive if there were any genuine belief structure lurking within.

19. the caution in public life that we unwittingly encourage is corrosive. those who say nothing wrong often say nothing much at all.

20. the pervasive intolerance of religious belief in certain circles must be resisted. the elite’s intolerance of religious belief is perhaps one of the most telling signs that its political project constitutes a less open intellectual movement than many within it would claim.

21. some cultures have produced vital advances; others remain dysfunctional and regressive. all cultures are now equal. criticism and value judgments are forbidden. yet this new dogma glosses over the fact that certain cultures and indeed subcultures . . . have produced wonders. others have proven middling, and worse, regressive and harmful.

22. we must resist the shallow temptation of a vacant and hollow pluralism. we, in america and more broadly the west, have for the past half century resisted 디파이ning national cultures in the name of inclusivity. but inclusion into what?

excerpts from the #1 new york times bestseller the technological republic: hard power, soft belief, and the future of the west, by alexander c. karp & nicholas w. zamiska

techrepublicbook.com



— https://nitter.net/palantirtech/status/2045574398573453312#m

news.title: softer al jazeera coverage in 거래소 for fewer iranian strikes? inside the alleged qatar–iran backchann
news.summary:
softer al jazeera coverage in 거래소 for fewer iranian strikes? inside the alleged qatar–iran backchannel deal

in recent days, reports have intensified about a possible behind-the-scenes understanding between qatar and iran in the final phase of the war—one that allegedly led to a reduction in iranian strikes, particularly after the major gas facility in northern qatar was hit.

however, a government source from a regional country reported that quiet dialogue did take place between qatar and iran, contributing to a noticeable drop in iranian strikes on doha compared to other gulf states in the closing days of the conflict.

according to the same source, iran also sought a less confrontational editorial line from al jazeera, along with greater airtime for iranian voices—reflecting the channel’s strategic importance for reaching arab and global audiences. this shift, the source claims, was indeed visible during the war.

one notable example is iranian analyst hassan ahmadian of the university of tehran, who appeared on both al jazeera’s arabic and english broadcasts.

news.title:
cz: hoping 암호화폐 will no longer be "treated specially" in 5 years  

in an april 9 interview with sco
news.summary:
cz: hoping 암호화폐 will no longer be "treated specially" in 5 years  

in an april 9 interview with scott melker, binance founder changpeng zhao (cz) @cz_binance  expressed his hope that within five years, 암호화폐 will no longer be viewed as a "special concept." just like the internet, people will stop discussing underlying technologies such as tcp/ip or html and simply use it. 

he believes 암호화폐 will natu랠리 become a financial tool and fintech money. meanwhile, 블록체인 applications will expand into areas like data storage, shifting the focus toward specific use cases rather than the technology itself. cz also noted that ai is accelerating industry growth, with ai agents heavily utilizing 암호화폐 to drive the implementation of more global applications. as for his own future, he stated he prefers to step back from frontline management and act as a "mentor or coach" to help other founders grow.

video

news.summary: source: binance.com/en/square/audio/…

news.title:
cz: even if i knew satoshi's identity, i would not reveal it 

on april 9, 2026, during an interview
news.summary:
cz: even if i knew satoshi's identity, i would not reveal it 

on april 9, 2026, during an interview with tbpn channel, changpeng zhao (cz) @cz_binance , founder of binance, was asked whETHer he knew the true identity of satoshi nakamoto. he stated that he does not know who satoshi nakamoto is, and would not disclose the information even if he did. 

cz mentioned that although he is curious about satoshi nakamoto, he believes that revealing satoshi’s identity would bring negative impacts and may lead to centralization issues for 비트코인. he added that 비트코인 remains more decentralized precisely because its founder’s identity is unknown and the founder is no longer involved in the project. therefore, he will not actively seek to uncover satoshi nakamoto’s true identity.

video

news.summary:
europe is in a full-blown energy crisis.

in fact, europe's energy crisis has gotten so bad that the european commission is now recommending europeans to work from home.

they are also recommending using public transportation to cut fossil fuel use.

as a result, many flights are expected to be cancelled on non-essential routes.

between the russia-ukraine war and the strait of hormuz closure, europe's vulnerability to energy supply shocks has been exposed.

visegrád 24 (@visegrad24)

on april 18th 1983, the organization which would later name itself hezbollah carried out a suicide bombing attack against the u.s. embassy in beirut, 63 people, including 17 americans.

a suicide bomber drove a chevrolet van packed with 2,000 pounds of explosives into the embassy.

the blast destroyed much of the front of the seven-story building.

63 people were killed, among them 17 americans (including several cia officers and a journalist).

the perpetrator was from the islamic jihad organization (ijo) but the real responsibility was with hezbollah and the islamic regime in iran. 

the islamic jihad organization was an early cover name and operational arm for what became hezbollah.

hezbollah itself did not publicly announce its existence until 1985, so it often operated under the ijo name in the early 1980s for plausible deniability.

u.s. investigations and intelligence assessments later attributed the attack to hezbollah, with direct support, financing, and direction from iran (via the islamic revolutionary guard corps and ministry of intelligence). 

hezbollah has historically denied direct responsibility for the 1983 embassy bombing and the related october 1983 marine barracks bombing in which 241 u.s. SOLdiers and 58 french SOLdiers were killed, but u.s. government assessments, declassified intelligence and expert analyses all say ijo attacks to hezbollah’s emerging network and their backers in tehran.





— https://nitter.net/visegrad24/status/2045739596307161157#m

news.title: alessandra sciurba, a director of an italian pro-immigration ngo, is marrying a convicted libyan mur
news.summary:
alessandra sciurba, a director of an italian pro-immigration ngo, is marrying a convicted libyan murderer and human trafficker. 🇮🇹🇱🇾 

the 31-year-old libyan alaa faraj, who was sentenced in 2016 to 30 years in prison for multiple murders as well for human trafficking of illegal immigrants (including the 2015 boat disaster which had 49 fatalities), received a partial pardon from the president of italy.

in june 2026, in palermo, he will marry the 47-year-old former president of the pro-immigration organization mediterranea saving humans. 

alessandra sciurba is an adjunct professor of law and a left-wing pro-migration activist from sicily, deeply involved in criticizing eu and italian border policies (as too harsh). 

the two met at ucciardone prison during educational workshops. 

the wedding will be an interfaith ceremony, which will be attended by an imam and the archbishop of palermo.
